title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.title: Authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) Recipe

Ingredients You'll Need for Adas Soup
How to Make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup: Step-by-Step
Pro Tips for the Best Adas Soup
seo.imageAlt: A comforting bowl of authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh herbs and lemon.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a hearty and flavorful bowl of homemade Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas), a Middle Eastern classic.

description: Experience the comforting embrace of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, a staple in Middle Eastern cuisine. This hearty and wholesome dish combines tender red lentils with aromatic spices, fresh lemon, and a hint of garlic, creating a truly satisfying and flavorful meal perfect for any occasion. It is a traditional and nourishing choice for any time of year.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain well. Chop the onion, mince the garlic, and dice the carrots and potatoes into small, uniform pieces to ensure even cooking.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Add Vegetables and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the diced carrots and potatoes, cooking for about 5 minutes until they begin to soften slightly. Add the ground cumin and turmeric (if using), stirring for 1 minute until aromatic. This toasts the spices and enhances their flavor.

instructions.title: Combine with Lentils and Liquid
instructions.text: Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, stirring to combine them with the vegetables and spices. Pour in 6 cups of vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer.

instructions.title: Simmer Until Tender
instructions.text: Allow the soup to simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. If the soup becomes too thick, add more broth or water until it reaches your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Season and Finish
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Season the soup with salt and black pepper to taste. Stir in the fresh lemon juice. For a smoother soup, you can use an immersion blender to blend a portion or all of the soup directly in the pot, or transfer to a regular blender in batches.

instructions.title: Serve Adas
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro or parsley. Serve immediately with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil and a side of warm pita bread or crunchy croutons.

tips: Always rinse lentils thoroughly to remove any debris and excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy.
For a richer flavor, toast the cumin and turmeric in the oil with the onions and garlic for a minute before adding liquids.
Adjust the consistency of the soup by adding more hot water or broth if it becomes too thick. A squeeze of extra lemon juice right before serving brightens the flavors.
While optional, blending a portion of the soup gives it a creamy texture without losing the heartiness of whole lentils and vegetables.
article:
A comforting bowl of soup holds a special place in cuisines worldwide, and in the heart of Middle Eastern cooking, few dishes evoke as much warmth and tradition as Adas. This beloved Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is more than just a meal; it's a staple, a remedy, and a taste of home for countless families across the Levant. Simple yet profoundly flavorful, Adas embodies the best of Middle Eastern soups and stews, offering a nourishing embrace with every spoonful. Whether you're seeking a hearty weeknight dinner, a healthy lunch, or a taste of authentic Lebanese hospitality, diving into the world of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup promises a delightful culinary journey.

## What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas)?

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known locally as "Adas bi Hamod" (lentils with lemon), is a quintessential dish from Lebanon and the broader Middle East. It’s celebrated for its vibrant flavor, creamy texture, and incredible nutritional value. Unlike some more elaborate stews, Adas is a testament to the power of simple, wholesome ingredients combined thoughtfully. Made primarily from red lentils, fresh lemon juice, garlic, and often a touch of cumin, it’s naturally vegan, gluten-free, and packed with plant-based protein and fiber.

Historically, Adas has been a cornerstone of the Lebanese diet, particularly during colder months or as a soothing meal when one feels under the weather. It’s incredibly economical to prepare, making it a beloved dish that transcends socioeconomic boundaries. Its sunny yellow hue, derived from the lentils and a squeeze of fresh lemon, is as inviting as its aroma. This soup perfectly showcases how humble ingredients can be transformed into something truly extraordinary, a hallmark of many Middle Eastern soups and stews.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Adas Soup

Creating an authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup requires a handful of fresh, quality ingredients. The beauty of Adas lies in its simplicity, allowing each component to shine. Here’s what you’ll typically need for a generous pot:

*   **Red Lentils (1.5 cups):** The star of the show. Make sure they are split red lentils, as they cook faster and break down more easily, contributing to the soup's creamy texture.
*   **Water or Vegetable Broth (6-8 cups):** Water is traditional, letting the lentil flavor shine. Broth adds an extra layer of savory depth.
*   **Onion (1 large):** Finely chopped, forms the aromatic base.
*   **Garlic (4-6 cloves):** Minced, provides essential pungent flavor.
*   **Olive Oil (3-4 tablespoons):** A good quality extra virgin olive oil is crucial for sautéing and drizzling.
*   **Fresh Lemon Juice (1/4 to 1/2 cup, or to taste):** The "hamod" (sourness) is non-negotiable, brightening the entire soup.
*   **Cumin (1-2 teaspoons):** Ground cumin enhances the earthy lentil flavor.
*   **Salt and Black Pepper (to taste):** Essential seasoning.
*   **Fresh Mint (a handful):** Finely chopped, for garnish and a burst of freshness. Dried mint can be used in a pinch, added during cooking.
*   **Optional additions:** A small potato or carrot (finely diced) can add body and a subtle sweetness.

## How to Make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup: Step-by-Step

Preparing this delicious Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is straightforward and rewarding. Follow these steps for a perfect pot of Adas:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent, but not browned. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it. If using, add diced potato or carrot here and sauté for a few minutes.
3.  **Add Lentils and Liquid:** Stir in the rinsed lentils and ground cumin. Cook for about 1 minute, stirring to coat the lentils with the aromatics and spices. Pour in the water or vegetable broth.
4.  **Simmer:** Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot partially (leaving a small gap for steam to escape), and simmer for 20-30 minutes, or until the lentils are completely soft and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Achieve Creaminess (Optional):** For an extra creamy texture, use an immersion blender to partially or fully blend the soup directly in the pot. Alternatively, scoop out a few ladlefuls, blend them in a regular blender, and return to the pot. Be careful when blending hot liquids.
6.  **Season and Finish:** Remove the soup from the heat. Stir in the fresh lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to taste. The amount of lemon juice is a personal preference, so start with less and add more until you reach your desired tanginess.
7.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped mint and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.

## Pro Tips for the Best Adas Soup

Elevating your Lebanese Red Lentil Soup from good to exceptional is all about paying attention to a few key details:

*   **Fresh Lemon is Key:** Do not skimp on fresh lemon juice. Its bright, acidic notes are what truly define Adas. Add it at the very end to preserve its vibrant flavor.
*   **Don't Overcook the Garlic:** Burnt garlic can impart a bitter taste to the entire soup. Keep an eye on it during the sautéing process.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** If you prefer a thicker soup, let it simmer uncovered for a bit longer, or add a little less liquid initially. For a thinner soup, simply add more hot water or broth.
*   **Embrace the Mint:** Fresh mint is not just a garnish; it adds a crucial layer of freshness that complements the earthiness of the lentils and the tang of the lemon. If you don't have fresh, a pinch of dried mint (rubbed between your palms to release its aroma) can be added during the simmering stage.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Always taste your soup before serving and adjust salt, pepper, and especially lemon juice. These final adjustments make all the difference.
*   **A Touch of Spice (Optional):** While traditional Adas is mild, a pinch of cayenne pepper or a tiny amount of Aleppo pepper can add a subtle warmth if you like a little heat.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is wonderful on its own, but it truly shines when served with traditional accompaniments:

*   **Pita Bread:** Warm pita bread is essential for dipping and scooping up every last drop of the delicious broth.
*   **Fresh Lemon Wedges:** Always offer extra lemon wedges on the side, allowing diners to adjust the tanginess to their liking.
*   **Fresh Herbs:** Beyond mint, a sprinkle of fresh parsley or cilantro can add extra color and herbaceous notes.
*   **Radishes and Green Onions:** A small plate of fresh radishes and green onions provides a crisp, peppery contrast to the creamy soup.
*   **Pickles (Mkhalal):** Assorted Middle Eastern pickles, especially pickled turnips, offer a delightful sour crunch that pairs beautifully with the soup.
*   **Side Salad:** A simple Lebanese salad (Fattoush or Tabbouleh) can make for a refreshing contrast, especially when serving Adas as part of a larger meal.
*   **Rice:** Some families enjoy serving Adas alongside a small portion of plain white rice, especially for a heartier meal.

## Variations and Dietary Adaptations

The versatility of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup allows for numerous adaptations:

*   **Vegan/Vegetarian:** Adas is naturally vegan and vegetarian. Ensure you use vegetable broth instead of chicken broth.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Red lentils are naturally gluten-free. Just ensure any broth used is also certified gluten-free.
*   **Added Vegetables:** Feel free to incorporate other finely diced vegetables like carrots, celery, or spinach during the simmering stage for added nutrients and flavor.
*   **Spice Profile:** Experiment with different spices. A pinch of turmeric can enhance the color and offer additional health benefits. A touch of coriander or allspice can also add depth.
*   **Tomato Paste:** For a slightly richer, tangier flavor and a deeper red hue, a tablespoon of tomato paste can be sautéed with the onions and garlic before adding the lentils.
*   **Green Lentils:** While red lentils are traditional for their quick cooking and creamy texture, green or brown lentils can also be used, though they will require a longer cooking time and may result in a less creamy soup.

## Storage and Reheating Instructions

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is an excellent candidate for meal prepping as it tastes even better the next day!

*   **Storage:** Allow the soup to cool completely before transferring it to airtight containers. Store in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
*   **Freezing:** Adas freezes beautifully. Portion the cooled soup into freezer-safe containers or bags and freeze for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator before reheating.
*   **Reheating:** Reheat the soup gently on the stovetop over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until heated through. If the soup has thickened considerably in the fridge, you may need to add a splash of water or broth to reach your desired consistency. Avoid boiling vigorously, as this can affect the texture of the lentils. Always taste and adjust seasonings, especially lemon juice, after reheating.

Embrace the comforting simplicity and rich flavors of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup. It's a dish that nourishes the body and soothes the soul, a true gem among Middle Eastern soups and stews.
